steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is “each one of the thousands of faux Chinese characters.” The key is the phrase “each one”—it's always singular, even though it refers to multiple characters. The descriptive phrase in dashes is a modifier that doesn't change the subject's singular number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: “Holds” is the only singular present-tense form. The rule of subject-verb agreement requires a singular verb, and only this choice satisfies it; the others are plural or wrong in tense. That matches choice B — B is correct.
